claim:

regimen and increasing her pain medication she improved.
evidence_preview: Switch to oral medication when appropriate.

 [**Hospital 2**] MEDICAL CONDITION:

claim:
You must wear your hard collar until you come back to the
office for follow up.
evidence_preview:
The cerebellar tonsils are
 again noted to lie below the level of the foramen magnum.

claim: You may remove it briefly for showering.
evidence_preview:
Neurologic: Neuro checks Q: 1 hr, Dilaudid PCA/Robaxan for pain,
   Zofran/Phenergan/Scop patch for nausea, C-collar for comfort only.

claim: No baths until your sutures are removed.
evidence_preview:
No
   dysmetria.

claim:
Have a friend/family member check your incision daily for
signs of infection.
evidence_preview: Cont family teaching.

claim: Take your pain medicine as prescribed.
evidence_preview:
As shift progressed pt no longer
   lethargic, c/o increased pain.

claim:
Exercise should be limited to walking; no lifting, straining,
or excessive bending.
evidence_preview:
Cardiovascular: Hydral prn to keep SBP<160, otherwise HD stable
   Pulmonary: O2 as needed, otherwise no issues
   Gastrointestinal / Abdomen: Should be able to start po today as
   tolerated
   Nutri

claim: You may wash your hair only after sutures have been removed.
evidence_preview: Gastrointestinal: NPO for now, will start diet after CT scan results.

claim:
You may shower before this time using a shower cap to cover
your head.
evidence_preview:
Response:
   Pt using PCA more frequently.

claim:
Increase your intake of fluids and fiber, as narcotic pain
medicine can cause constipation.
evidence_preview:
As shift progressed pt no longer
   lethargic, c/o increased pain.

claim:
We generally recommend taking
an over the counter stool softener, such as Docusate (Colace)
while taking narcotic pain medication.
evidence_preview: Switch to oral medication when appropriate.

claim:
Unless directed by your doctor, do not take any
anti-inflammatory medicines such as Motrin, Aspirin, Advil, and
Ibuprofen etc.
evidence_preview: Not on pressors.

claim:
Clearance to drive and return to work will be addressed at
your post-operative office visit.
evidence_preview:
At start of shift, pt admitting to good pain management
 pt given mult
   doses of morphine prior to iniation of dilaudid pca.
